channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: After the initial crush of personnel announcements for President-elect Donald Trump's incoming administration, now the nominations process officially begins. Senate hearings are scheduled this coming week for several of Trump's picks for the Cabinet. Many have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States are preparing to help or thwart Trump's second-term plans
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office, many elected officials in Republican and Democratic states are preparing to either aid or oppose his policies.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb And Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DNC chair candidates attempt to stand out at first forum
item.description: The eight people vying for the position of chair were featured last and while there was no clear winner, there was definitely a loser.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Political experts discuss Trump-Newsom relationship, political fallout for LA Mayor Bass amid wildfires
item.description: (INSIDE CALIFORNIA POLITICS) — The Los Angeles County wildfires have claimed several lives and burned thousands of structures have also caused a stir in the political sphere. San Francisco Chronicle columnist Emily Hoeven spoke with Inside California Politics about how L.A.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina's new Democratic governor pledges to rebuild after Helene, work with GOP
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina Gov. Josh Stein kicked off his term in office Saturday by praising the resilience of the state's residents following Hurricane Helene and pledging to “cut through red tape to help people rebuild quicker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden honors Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom
item.description: President Joe Biden has honored Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom with distinction.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Congress braces for California wildfire fallout
item.description: House lawmakers are bracing for what could be a contentious battle over emergency spending in the wake of the wildfires that are tearing through Southern California — an unprecedented disaster that’s already estimated to have caused more than $50 billion in damage in and around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Many Democrats don't think they'll see a woman become president, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: An increasing number of Democrats are questioning whether their party’s commitment to diverse candidates, especially women, may lead to more political struggles in the Trump era.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Some US lawmakers want more Christianity in the classroom. Trump could embolden their plans
item.description: Conservative lawmakers across the U.S. are pushing to introduce more Christianity to public school classrooms, testing the separation of church and state.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden is still considering pardons for people who have been criticized or threatened by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Friday said he was still considering whether to give pardons to people who have been criticized or threatened by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden claim about empty hydrants undermined by LA water utility's lack of common wildfire safety protocol
item.description: President Biden on Thursday said that empty fire hydrants in the Los Angeles area were the result of an intentional power shutoff, but a report indicates the city's water utility might lack a common safety protocol.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana AG asks Supreme Court to uphold law requiring parental consent for a minor's abortion
item.description: Montana AG Austin Knudsen petitioned the U.S. Supreme Court to uphold a parental consent law for minors' abortions that his state's high court struck down last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: The administrator of the Panama Canal has responded to criticisms from U.S. President-elect Donald Trump, denying that there is any interference from China in the canal’s operations and saying giving special privileges to any nation would only cause problems.
item.dc:creator: Alma Solís, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mitchell bid to delay burglary trial is latest twist in presession runup for Minnesota lawmakers
item.description: With the Capitol power situation already cloudy, a DFL state senator facing a burglary trial is asking that it be postponed. The tied Senate and closely divided Minnesota could face startup problems next week.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: US streamlines deportation flights to China
item.description: The Biden administration repatriated more than 500 Chinese nationals in the last year, laying the groundwork for systematic deportations across the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden extends time in US for 800,000 Venezuelans, Salvadorans as Trump readies immigration crackdown
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — About 600,000 Venezuelans and more than 230,000 Salvadorans already living in the United States can legally remain another 18 months, the Department of Homeland Security said Friday, barely a week before President-elect Donald Trump takes office with promises of hardline immigration pol
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court dismisses ousted Hillsborough State Attorney's lawsuit against DeSantis
item.description: A federal appeals court dismissed a lawsuit brought against Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis by Andrew Warren, a former state attorney he suspended from his position.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump vows to shed light on mystery drones when president
item.description: Trump, who will be inaugurated as the 47th president on Jan. 20, criticized the Biden administration's handling of the mystery drones.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump can still vote after sentencing, but can't own a gun and will have to turn over DNA sample
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump doesn't have to go to jail, pay a fine or perform community service as a result of his New York hush money conviction. A judge ended the case Friday with a sentence of an unconditional discharge, closing the case with no punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Investigation clears Duluth mayor’s campaign manager and partner
item.description: An external investigation commissioned by the city of Duluth has found Mayor Roger Reinert’s partner and campaign manager Amber Gurske did not act improperly through her communications with city staff early in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: President-elect Trump to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a singular moment in U.S. history, President-elect Donald Trump faces sentencing Friday for his New York hush money conviction after the nation's highest court refused to intervene.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A sharply divided Supreme Court has rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Friends, family, presidents pay tribute to Jimmy Carter at funeral
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As they filed into the front pews at Washington National Cathedral, wearing dark suits and mostly solemn faces, five current and former presidents came together for Jimmy Carter's funeral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Homeless mental health and affordability headline NYC mayor’s State of the City address
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams on Thursday laid out an ambitious agenda for 2025 while fighting for his political life. His State of the City address was the last of his first term in office as he faces a tough reelection battle.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Sen. J.D. Vance resigns ahead of vice presidential inauguration
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- U.S. Sen. J.D. Vance resigned from his seat in Congress' upper chamber on Thursday ahead of his inauguration as vice president to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senator: Alito has made impropriety the norm
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.), a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, on Thursday blasted conservative Justice Samuel Alito for having a conversation with President-elect Trump amidst Trump’s effort to have courts halt his criminal sentencing in New York.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Lack of federal funds may mean no EBT replacements for scam victims
item.description: Many parents have reported benefits being erased from EBT cards in the weeks since Congress declined to continue funding.
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, NewsNation

item.title: These Democrats flipped their votes on the Laken Riley Act
item.description: Seven House Democrats flipped their votes to "yes" on a GOP-led bill named after the slain Georgia student Laken Riley that would require detention of migrants arrested for theft.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about Trump and his keen interest in Greenland
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said multiple times that the U.S. should buy Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. The sparsely populated island is geopolitically important and mineral-rich.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump’s latest attempt to get Friday’s hush money sentencing called off
item.description: In a one-sentence ruling following an emergency hearing, Judge Ellen Gesmer denied Trump’s request for an immediate order that would spare him from being sentenced while he appeals Judge Juan M. Merchan’s decision last week to uphold the historic verdict.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit after two years of shortfalls
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: Tran Nguyen, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Politicians reflect on January 6, shifting landscape of power transitions
item.description: The 2024 Presidential Election results have been officially certified by members of Congress. While the session typically happens without fanfare, there was extra security in place this time.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
